message:
fix(runtime): harden fault containment, captured-state lifecycle, and parallel scheduler

This commit addresses several interrelated correctness issues in the
runtime scheduler paths that surface under fault-containment policies
other than POISON_REGION (e.g., FAIL_FAST, ERROR_ONLY).

Captured-state release refactor (lifecycle.c, runtime_internal.h, scheduler.c):
- Extract asx_task_release_capture_internal() from the scheduler-local
  static asx_task_release_capture() and promote it to an internal API
  declared in runtime_internal.h.  This is required because the parallel
  scheduler also needs to release captured state on task completion, and
  duplicating the logic would create a maintenance hazard.
- All four task-completion paths in both schedulers (OK, ERR, cancel
  force-complete, finalising) now call the shared release function.

Fault containment in scheduler (scheduler.c):
- When a task poll returns an error, the scheduler now calls
  asx_region_contain_fault() and, if the active policy is NOT
  POISON_REGION, propagates the fault status to the caller instead of
  silently discarding it.  Previously the return value was cast to void,
  which meant FAIL_FAST mode never actually failed fast.

Parallel scheduler hardening (parallel.c):
- Mirror the fault-containment propagation logic from the core scheduler.
- Move poll-count bookkeeping (polls_this_lane, polls_total, any_polled)
  to immediately after the poll call and before the completion-path
  branches, so that the counters are accurate even when a task completes
  or errors on its first poll.
- Add stale-handle refresh: before polling a task, reconstruct its
  packed handle from the live slot state to avoid operating on a handle
  whose state-mask bits are stale from a previous round.
- Remove invalid slot indices from lanes via lane_remove_internal()
  instead of silently skipping them, preventing phantom tasks from
  accumulating across rounds.
- Extract parallel_return_budget() and parallel_return_quiescent()
  helpers that emit trace events before returning, ensuring that budget
  exhaustion and quiescence are always observable in the trace stream.
- Add early quiescence check after the per-round lane sweep so the
  scheduler exits promptly when all tasks complete mid-round.
- Remove trailing blank line at EOF.

Quiescence drain (quiescence.c):
- Add asx_region_has_uncancelled_tasks() check: when a region is in
  CLOSING state with live tasks that have NOT yet been cancelled, issue
  a second cancel propagation pass.  This fixes a race where
  asx_region_drain() could stall if cancel_pending was not set on all
  tasks before the drain budget loop began.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -81,4 +81,7 @@ ASX_MUST_USE asx_status asx_task_slot_lookup(asx_task_id id, asx_task_slot **out
 ASX_MUST_USE asx_status asx_obligation_slot_lookup(asx_obligation_id id,
                                                    asx_obligation_slot **out);
 
+/* Release captured state for a task exactly once. */
+void asx_task_release_capture_internal(asx_task_slot *task);
+
 #endif /* ASX_RUNTIME_INTERNAL_H */
